title: Multiplayer Drawing Room with Voice Messaging

requirements.summary: Implement real-time collaborative drawing with stroke polling and rendering
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend polls for new strokes at a short interval (e.g., 1–2 seconds) and renders them on the shared canvas
Multiple users drawing simultaneously results in all strokes appearing for all participants

file_operations.description: Add useAddStroke mutation hook that calls backend addStroke with roomCode and stroke data. Add usePollStrokes query hook that fetches strokes for a room with a 2-second refetch interval, similar to existing pollMessage pattern. These hooks will enable collaborative drawing by submitting local strokes and continuously fetching new strokes from other participants.

Create a collaborative canvas component that wraps DrawingCanvas functionality and adds multiplayer capabilities. It will accept a roomCode prop, use usePollStrokes to fetch and render remote strokes on the canvas, and use useAddStroke to submit local strokes as they are drawn. The component will maintain a set of already-rendered stroke IDs to avoid duplicates and will render both local and remote strokes on the same canvas context.

requirements.summary: Implement in-room voice messaging with recording, base64 encoding, and playback
acceptanceCriteria: A record button is visible in the room UI; pressing and holding records audio, releasing stops recording
The recorded audio blob is base64-encoded and sent to the backend via a sendVoiceMessage function
All room participants polling for messages receive new voice messages and can play them back via an audio element
Voice messages appear in a chronological list alongside a playback control

file_operations.description: Add useSendVoiceMessage mutation hook that accepts roomCode, audioData (base64 string), and sender, then calls backend addAudioMessage. Add usePollVoiceMessages query hook that fetches audio messages for a room with a 3-second refetch interval, consistent with the existing pollMessage pattern. These hooks enable voice message submission and real-time retrieval.

Create a custom hook that manages MediaRecorder lifecycle for voice recording. It will expose startRecording, stopRecording, isRecording state, and return the recorded audio blob as a base64-encoded string. The hook will handle browser media permissions, MediaRecorder instantiation with WebM/Opus codec preference, and blob-to-base64 conversion using FileReader.

Create a voice message panel component that displays a record button (hold to record, release to stop) using the useAudioRecorder hook, and shows a scrollable list of voice messages fetched via usePollVoiceMessages. Each message in the list will include sender name, timestamp, and a play button that renders an HTML audio element with the base64 data URI. Style the panel with pastel colors and doodle aesthetics consistent with existing UI.

requirements.summary: Build a unified Room UI with lobby, canvas, voice panel, and participant info
acceptanceCriteria: Home page shows 'Create Room' and 'Join Room' options
Join Room accepts a room code input
Room page displays a full collaborative canvas matching the existing DrawingCanvas component style
Room page shows a voice message section with a record button and scrollable list of received voice messages
Room code is displayed on the room page so users can share it
Visual style is consistent with the existing pastel/doodle aesthetic

file_operations.description: Replace the existing sender/recipient pairing flow with a room-based lobby. Add a 'Create Room' button that prompts for a room name, calls useCreateRoom mutation, and navigates to /room/:code on success. Add a 'Join Room' section with a text input for room code and a join button that navigates to /room/:code. Remove references to sender/recipient roles. Maintain the existing pastel blob decorations and doodle background aesthetic.

Add useCreateRoom mutation hook that calls backend createRoom with a roomName and returns the generated room code. Add useJoinRoom mutation hook that calls backend joinRoom with roomCode and a username. Add useGetParticipantCount query hook (optional, if backend exposes participant count). These hooks enable room creation and joining flows from the frontend.

Create the main room page component that accepts roomCode from route params. The layout will include: a header displaying the room code with a copy button and participant count, the CollaborativeCanvas component (created in REQ-2) occupying the main area with color/stroke controls, and the VoiceMessagePanel component (created in REQ-3) in a side panel or bottom section. Use flexbox or grid layout to organize these sections. Apply pastel colors, rounded corners, and doodle shadows consistent with the existing theme from frontend/src/index.css and frontend/tailwind.config.js. On mount, call useJoinRoom to register the user in the room.

requirements.summary: Update TanStack Router routes to include /room/:code and update App.tsx
acceptanceCriteria: Route /room/:code renders the room page with the correct room code
Navigating to / shows the lobby with Create/Join options
No broken routes from previous sender/recipient flow (or they are cleanly removed if replaced)

file_operations.description: Add a new route for /room/:code that renders the Room page component. Remove or comment out the /sender and /recipient routes since they are being replaced by the unified room flow. Ensure the / route still renders the updated Home page (modified in REQ-4). Maintain the existing Layout wrapper and Toaster configuration.
